evidence.support: The AWAN processing architecture is described.
The spectral and CSS losses are defined.
Self-ensemble and multi-model ensemble procedures are described.

value: Extract shallow features from the RGB input with convolution
Process features through stacked dual residual attention blocks
Apply adaptive weighted channel attention
Apply patch-level second-order non-local spatial modeling
Predict a 31-band hyperspectral image
Compute supervised spectral reconstruction loss
When CSS is known, project the reconstructed HSI back to RGB and add the CSS consistency loss
Optionally apply self-ensemble and multi-model ensemble at evaluation

evidence.support: The paper's contribution list names the network, modules, CSS prior, and challenge performance.
The conclusion restates the model and performance claims.

value: Adaptive Weighted Attention Network for spectral reconstruction
Dual residual attention backbone with long and short skip connections
Adaptive weighted channel attention
Patch-level second-order non-local spatial-context module
CSS-based RGB consistency constraint
First place on NTIRE2020 Clean and third place on Real World

evidence.support: The CSS operator forms the RGB consistency loss.
The constraint is limited to tracks with known CSS.

calibration_design.value: Camera consistency is enforced using the benchmark-provided CSS function to project ground-truth and reconstructed HSIs into RGB space. No blind camera calibration is performed.

evidence.support: The source identifies known and unknown camera-response tracks.
The authors explicitly state that CSS can only be introduced for the Clean track.

value: The CSS consistency constraint requires a known camera spectral-sensitivity function.
Different benchmark tracks may use different sensitivity functions.
When camera response is unknown, the CSS constraint cannot be applied.

evidence.support: The benchmark track generation and spectral sampling are specified.

capture_protocol.review_notes: This is a benchmark data-generation protocol, not an operational mobile capture procedure.

value.clean_track: Noise-free RGB generated with a known camera spectral-sensitivity function
value.real_world_track: JPEG-compressed RGB generated through an unknown camera response function
value.spectral_sampling: 31 bands from 400 to 700 nm at approximately 10 nm intervals

chromophore_representation.review_notes: Melanin, hemoglobin, and other skin chromophores are not represented.

evidence.support: The AWAN backbone, DRABs, convolutional layers, skip connections, and residual design are described.
The AWCA and PSNL mechanisms are defined.

value.mapping: Three-channel RGB image to 31-channel hyperspectral image

evidence.support: The NTIRE2018 and NTIRE2020 dataset sizes, dimensions, bands, and tracks are reported.
The AWCA and PSNL equations are provided.
The combined loss, HSI loss, CSS loss, MRAE, and RMSE equations are defined.

value: Adaptive weighted pooling for channel descriptors
Sigmoid-gated channel attention rescaling
Second-order non-local spatial attention and residual refinement
Combined loss l = l_h + tau l_r
Hyperspectral MRAE loss between ground truth and reconstruction
CSS reprojection discrepancy between ground-truth and reconstructed HSI
MRAE evaluation metric
RMSE evaluation metric

evidence.support: MRAE and RMSE are formally defined.
Comparative tables, runtime, visual errors, and spectral curves are used.

value: Mean relative absolute error
Root mean square error
Runtime
Official challenge rank
Visual reconstruction error maps
Spectral response-curve agreement

illumination_assumptions.review_notes: The source does not define, estimate, or recover scene illumination or illuminant spectral power distribution.

evidence.support: The Clean and Real World camera-response conditions are explicitly distinguished.

instrument_or_camera.value: The benchmark supplies camera-response conditions rather than identifying a single physical camera model. Clean tracks provide known CSS functions; Real World tracks use unknown camera response functions and JPEG-compressed RGB images.

evidence.support: The task is formulated as mapping RGB pixels to hyperspectral signals.
Training uses RGB-HSI pairs and the known CSS function where available.

value: Three-channel RGB image
Camera spectral-sensitivity function when known
Ground-truth hyperspectral image during supervised training

evidence.support: The datasets, reconstructed HSI, CSS projection, MRAE, and RMSE are defined.

value: RGB pixel values
Recovered hyperspectral radiance values
Ground-truth hyperspectral values
RGB reprojection discrepancy under the camera sensitivity function
Mean relative absolute error
Root mean square error

evidence.support: The paper states that RGB can be generated by applying a known CSS function to the reconstructed HSI.
The CSS projection operator is included in the reconstruction loss.

physical_model.review_notes: This is a camera spectral projection constraint, not a scene illumination or skin light-transport model.
physical_model.value: A camera projection relationship in which a reconstructed hyperspectral image is mapped back to RGB through the supplied camera spectral-sensitivity function.

evidence.support: The abstract and introduction define RGB-to-HSI spectral reconstruction and state that the inverse process is severely ill-posed.

problem_addressed.value: Recovering a high-dimensional hyperspectral image from a three-channel RGB image. The inverse mapping is severely ill-posed because multiple spectra can produce the same RGB observation.

evidence.support: The inverse ambiguity is stated.
Known and unknown camera-response conditions are distinguished.
The CSS prior is explicitly restricted to the Clean track.

value: The RGB-to-HSI inverse problem is severely ill-posed.
The CSS prior requires a known camera spectral-sensitivity function.
The CSS prior cannot be applied to the Real World benchmark because its camera response is unknown.
The method is trained and evaluated on benchmark camera-response conditions and does not infer an unseen device response.
The paper does not address illumination variation as a separate latent variable.

evidence.support: Validation MRAE and RMSE values and official test values are tabulated.
AWCA and CSS ablation improvements are reported.
Comparative results and official ranks are reported.

value.CSS_ablation: Adding the CSS consistency term improves Clean-track reconstruction accuracy.
value.qualitative_result: Recovered spectra and error maps are reported as closer to ground truth than the compared methods.

evidence.support: The benchmark consists of natural HSIs and RGB images.

sample_or_subject_population.value: Natural-scene hyperspectral images and their corresponding RGB projections. No human-subject or skin-subject population is reported.

scattering_representation.review_notes: Tissue or surface scattering is not modeled.

evidence.support: The abstract states the AWAN, AWCA, PSNL, and CSS-prior objectives.
The contribution list describes the network modules and CSS consistency constraint.

scientific_objective.value: Improve RGB-to-HSI reconstruction by combining a deep adaptive attention network with channel and spatial-context modeling and, when available, a known camera spectral-sensitivity consistency constraint.

skin_representation.review_notes: The study uses general natural-scene hyperspectral datasets and does not define a skin-specific representation.

evidence.support: The camera-response consistency mechanism is explicit.
The unknown-camera boundary is explicit.

value.boundary: AWAN assumes a supplied CSS function when using its camera-consistency prior. It explicitly cannot apply that prior when camera response is unknown. It does not estimate illumination, camera response, skin chromophores, scattering, or capture observability.
directly_relevant_mechanisms: RGB reprojection consistency from reconstructed spectra

evidence.support: Ablations evaluate PSNL, AWCA, and CSS.
Comparative and ensemble evaluations are described.

validation_types: Comparison against six prior spectral-reconstruction methods

title: Adaptive Weighted Attention Network with Camera Spectral Sensitivity Prior for Spectral Reconstruction from RGB Images
